description: Phase A of 3: Manual LAN federation for claude-peers-mcp. Enables Claude Code sessions on different LAN machines to discover each other and exchange messages by manually connecting federation agents via IP address. Includes TLS-encrypted communication (Ed25519), PSK authentication, HMAC message signing, subnet restriction, peer list sync, cross-machine message relay, and CLI commands. Assumes broker auth (prd-broker-auth.md) and structured messages (prd-structured-messages.md) are already implemented. BUG FIXES applied from PAL consensus + codereview: (1) federation endpoints split into LAN-facing (PSK auth) and local-facing (bearer auth), (2) remote send uses broker HTTP endpoint /federation/send-to-remote (server.ts and broker.ts are SEPARATE PROCESSES — cannot share memory or make in-process calls), (3) HMAC canonicalization limitation documented, (4) Ed25519 with RSA fallback, (5) sync error handling, (6) CLI commands route through broker endpoints.

userStories.title: Federation TypeScript types
userStories.description: As a developer, I want shared TypeScript types for the federation layer so that all federation modules have a consistent, type-safe contract.
acceptanceCriteria: FederationConfig interface added to shared/types.ts with fields: enabled (boolean), port (number), subnet (string CIDR), certPath (string), keyPath (string)

RemotePeer interface added with fields: id (string, hostname:original_id), machine (string), cwd (string), git_root (string | null), session_name (string), summary (string), last_seen (string ISO timestamp)
FederationHandshakeRequest interface added with fields: psk (string), hostname (string), version (string)

FederationRelayRequest interface added with fields: from_id (string), from_machine (string), to_id (string), text (string), type (optional string), metadata (optional Record<string, unknown>), reply_to (optional number), signature (string HMAC-SHA256)
FederationPeersResponse interface added with fields: hostname (string), peers (Peer[])
ListPeersRequest.scope extended to include 'lan' as a valid value
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Auto-generate self-signed TLS certificate
userStories.description: As a user starting federation for the first time, I want a TLS certificate to be auto-generated so that encrypted communication works without manual PKI setup.
acceptanceCriteria: New file federation.ts created with cert generation functions

On first federation startup, if no cert/key files exist at configured paths, generate a self-signed X.509 certificate. Prefer Ed25519: openssl genpkey -algorithm Ed25519 -out key.pem && openssl req -new -x509 -key key.pem -out cert.pem -days 365 -subj '/CN=$(hostname)'. If Ed25519 fails (e.g. older LibreSSL on macOS), fall back to RSA-2048: openssl req -newkey rsa:2048 -noenc -keyout key.pem -x509 -days 365 -out cert.pem -subj '/CN=$(hostname)'
Default paths: ~/.claude-peers-federation.crt and ~/.claude-peers-federation.key
Configurable via CLAUDE_PEERS_FEDERATION_CERT and CLAUDE_PEERS_FEDERATION_KEY env vars
Certificate Subject CN set to machine hostname (normalized: lowercase, truncated >63 chars, colons rejected at startup), validity 365 days
Key file written with mode 0600
If cert/key already exist, they are loaded without regeneration
Logs: '[CPM-federation] TLS cert loaded from <path>' (never logs key material)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PAL consensus (4 models, avg 8.25/10) confirmed Ed25519 over RSA-2048: faster (~1ms vs ~200ms), smaller keys, modern. Verified locally with Bun.

userStories.title: Enable federation on a machine
userStories.description: As a user, I want to enable federation via environment variable so that my broker participates in LAN peer networking.
acceptanceCriteria: Setting CLAUDE_PEERS_FEDERATION_ENABLED=true causes the federation agent to start alongside the broker in broker.ts
When federation is disabled (default: false), no LAN-facing port is opened, no federation code executes, and existing behavior is identical to pre-federation
Federation agent starts a TLS server on the port specified by CLAUDE_PEERS_FEDERATION_PORT (default: 7900) using Bun.serve() with tls option
Federation agent binds to 0.0.0.0 (all interfaces) to accept LAN connections
On startup, federation agent logs: '[CPM-federation] Listening on 0.0.0.0:<port> (TLS)'
On startup with federation disabled, no federation log lines are emitted
Federation startup is wrapped in try/catch for graceful degradation if TLS or port binding fails
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: GOTCHA: Wrap entire federation startup in try/catch. If TLS cert is invalid or port is in use, log error and continue with broker operating in localhost-only mode.

userStories.title: Subnet restriction for federation connections
userStories.description: As a security-conscious user, I want federation to only accept connections from my local subnet so that machines outside my trusted network cannot connect.
acceptanceCriteria: CLAUDE_PEERS_FEDERATION_SUBNET env var specifies allowed CIDR (e.g., 192.168.1.0/24)
Default: auto-detect the local machine's /24 subnet from the primary network interface using 'ip route show default' as primary method (os.networkInterfaces() returns wrong 172.x.x.x on WSL2)
Incoming TLS connections from IPs outside the allowed subnet are immediately rejected before handshake
Federation agent logs rejected connections: '[CPM-federation] Rejected connection from <ip> (outside subnet <cidr>)'
Outgoing connections (via federation connect) also verify the target IP is within the allowed subnet
Subnet check uses standard CIDR bitmasking, supports both /24 and arbitrary prefix lengths
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: GOTCHA: WSL2 uses os.networkInterfaces() returning 172.x.x.x (internal NAT). Use 'ip route show default' as primary detection, fall back to os.networkInterfaces(). Tailscale /32 routes need special handling in subnet detection.

userStories.title: HMAC-SHA256 message signing for federation relay
userStories.description: As a security-conscious user, I want all federation messages to be signed so that tampering is detected even if TLS is somehow compromised.
acceptanceCriteria: signMessage(body, psk) function computes HMAC-SHA256 of canonical JSON (Object.keys(body).sort() — top-level keys only) keyed with PSK
verifySignature(body, signature, psk) validates HMAC using crypto.timingSafeEqual() for constant-time comparison
Every message relayed via /federation/relay must include X-Message-Signature header
Receiving federation agent validates HMAC before accepting; invalid signatures return { error: 'Invalid message signature' } with HTTP 403
Messages without signatures are rejected with { error: 'Missing message signature' } with HTTP 403

Code comment documents that Object.keys().sort() only sorts top-level keys (nested objects are serialized as-is). This is acceptable because FederationRelayRequest has a flat structure with only one nested field (metadata) whose internal order is not security-sensitive.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: BUG FIX #3: PAL consensus identified that Object.keys().sort() only sorts top-level keys. Documented as known limitation. Acceptable for flat FederationRelayRequest structure. If nested canonicalization is ever needed, switch to recursive key sort.

userStories.title: Federation broker endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer building the federation layer, I want the local broker to expose federation-specific endpoints so that the federation agent can query local state and deliver remote messages.
acceptanceCriteria: LAN-FACING endpoints (called by remote federation agents over TLS): POST /federation/peers returns all local peers for federation sync, POST /federation/relay accepts { from_id, from_machine, to_id, text, type?, metadata?, reply_to? } and inserts into local messages table. Both require PSK auth via X-Claude-Peers-PSK header ONLY (not bearer token — remote machines don't have it).

LOCAL-FACING endpoints (called by cli.ts and server.ts over localhost): POST /federation/send-to-remote accepts { to_id, text, type?, metadata?, reply_to? } and routes through the in-process federation agent to the correct remote. POST /federation/connect accepts { host, port } to connect to a remote. POST /federation/disconnect accepts { host, port } to disconnect. GET /federation/status returns federation state. All require bearer token auth (same as other local endpoints).

The broker's existing POST /list-peers endpoint is extended: when scope='lan' and federation is enabled, it returns BOTH local peers (from SQLite) and remote peers (from the federation agent's in-memory cache, merged by the broker since both run in the same process).
LAN-facing federation endpoints are NOT rate-limited (federation agent is trusted)
Local-facing federation endpoints follow existing rate limiting
All federation endpoints return 404 if federation is not enabled (CLAUDE_PEERS_FEDERATION_ENABLED is not true)
PID liveness check (process.kill(pid, 0)) is bypassed for remote from_ids (those containing colon separator). Remote PIDs are not meaningful locally.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: CRITICAL ARCHITECTURE NOTE: broker.ts and server.ts run as SEPARATE PROCESSES (server.ts spawns broker via Bun.spawn). They communicate via HTTP to localhost:7899. The federation agent runs IN-PROCESS with broker.ts (second Bun.serve()), so broker.ts CAN access federation state directly. But server.ts and cli.ts CANNOT — they must use HTTP endpoints. Two endpoint categories: (1) LAN-facing (PSK auth, called by remote federation agents), (2) Local-facing (bearer auth, called by server.ts/cli.ts).

userStories.title: Connect to a remote federation agent by IP
userStories.description: As a user, I want to manually connect to a peer machine's federation agent by IP address so that our brokers can share peer lists and route messages.
acceptanceCriteria: New CLI command: bun cli.ts federation connect <host>:<port>
CLI sends POST /federation/connect { host, port } to the local broker (bearer token auth). The broker's in-process federation agent then initiates the TLS connection to the remote.
Federation agent connects to remote host:port with tls: { rejectUnauthorized: false } (self-signed certs expected)
On TLS handshake, sends PSK from ~/.claude-peers-token in X-Claude-Peers-PSK header via HTTP POST to remote /federation/handshake
Remote agent validates PSK using crypto.timingSafeEqual(); mismatch returns { error: 'PSK mismatch' } and closes connection
If PSK matches, both sides store each other as a federated peer (in-memory, not persisted to SQLite)
CLI prints: 'Connected to <host>:<port> (<remote_hostname>). <N> remote peers available.'
If connection fails (network error, PSK mismatch, TLS error), CLI prints a clear error message
Connection timeout: 5 seconds
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: GOTCHA: fetch() TLS with rejectUnauthorized: false verified working in Bun (non-standard but functional for self-signed certs).

userStories.title: Disconnect from a remote federation agent
userStories.description: As a user, I want to disconnect from a specific remote federation agent so that I can control which machines are in my peer network.
acceptanceCriteria: New CLI command: bun cli.ts federation disconnect <host>:<port>
CLI sends POST /federation/disconnect { host, port } to the local broker (bearer token auth). The broker's in-process federation agent handles teardown.
Removes the remote from the federated peers list
Closes the TLS connection to that remote
Remote peers from that machine are removed from list_peers(scope='lan') results
CLI prints: 'Disconnected from <host>:<port>.'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Periodic peer list sync with remote brokers
userStories.description: As a connected federation agent, I want to periodically sync peer lists with remote brokers so that new sessions on remote machines are discovered automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Every 30 seconds (configurable), federation agent fetches peer list from each connected remote via POST /federation/peers
Remote peers stored in an in-memory map keyed by <remote_host>:<remote_port>:<peer_id>
Remote peers include a machine field containing the remote hostname (or IP if hostname unavailable)
Stale remote peers (from a remote that hasn't synced in 90 seconds) are automatically removed
Local peers remain managed by the local broker (SQLite); remote peers are never written to the local SQLite database
Peer list sync requests are authenticated with PSK (same as handshake)
Network errors during sync must be caught and logged as warnings; they must NOT crash the broker. Failed syncs count against the 90-second stale timeout.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: GOTCHA: A naive fetch() call will throw unhandled promise rejection if the remote goes offline. Wrap sync loop in try/catch, log warning, continue. Failed syncs still count toward 90s staleness.

userStories.title: List peers with LAN scope in server.ts
userStories.description: As a Claude Code session, I want to list peers across the LAN so that I can discover sessions on other machines.
acceptanceCriteria: list_peers MCP tool gains new scope value 'lan'
server.ts passes scope='lan' to the broker's existing POST /list-peers endpoint (the broker handles merging local SQLite peers with federation agent's in-memory remote peers — see US-006)
Remote peers are distinguished with [REMOTE:<hostname>] prefix on first line of each entry
Remote peers include: ID (prefixed with hostname, e.g., rafi-mac:a1b2c3d4), CWD, git_root, session_name, summary, last_seen, machine hostname
Remote peers do NOT include PID or TTY (not meaningful across machines)
When federation is disabled, scope='lan' returns error: 'Federation is not enabled. Set CLAUDE_PEERS_FEDERATION_ENABLED=true to use LAN scope.'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Send message to a remote peer
userStories.description: As a Claude Code session, I want to send a message to a peer on another machine so that I can collaborate across machines.
acceptanceCriteria: send_message in server.ts transparently detects remote peer IDs (those with hostname prefix containing colon, e.g., rafi-mac:b2c3d4e5)

For remote peers, server.ts sends the message to the broker's local POST /federation/send-to-remote endpoint (bearer token auth, defined in US-006). The broker then routes it through its in-process federation agent.
The broker's federation agent forwards message to the correct remote federation agent via POST /federation/relay (TLS, PSK-authenticated, HMAC-signed)
Remote federation agent delivers message to its local broker via POST /send-message (localhost, bearer token)
Remote peer receives message via normal polling/channel push mechanism (no changes to recipient-side delivery)
Cross-machine messages include from_machine in the channel notification meta
If remote federation agent is unreachable, send_message returns error: 'Remote machine <hostname> is unreachable'
Message size limit (10KB) is enforced locally before relaying
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: ARCHITECTURE: server.ts and broker.ts are SEPARATE PROCESSES. server.ts cannot call federation functions directly. It sends to the broker's /federation/send-to-remote endpoint (bearer auth), and the broker routes through its in-process federation agent.

userStories.title: Federation status CLI command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the current federation status so that I know which machines are connected and how many remote peers are available.
acceptanceCriteria: New CLI command: bun cli.ts federation status
CLI sends GET /federation/status to the local broker (bearer token auth). The broker returns federation state from its in-process federation agent.
Displays: federation enabled/disabled, listening port, connected remotes (host, port, hostname, remote peer count, connection uptime), total remote peers
If federation is disabled, prints: 'Federation is disabled. Set CLAUDE_PEERS_FEDERATION_ENABLED=true to enable.'
If federation is enabled but no remotes connected, prints: 'Federation enabled on port <port>. No remote machines connected.'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Federation agent test suite
userStories.description: As a developer, I want comprehensive tests for the federation agent so that LAN networking regressions are caught early.
acceptanceCriteria: New test file: federation.test.ts
Test: federation agent starts TLS server on configured port
Test: /federation/handshake with correct PSK returns success
Test: /federation/handshake with wrong PSK returns error
Test: /federation/peers returns local peer list (PSK-authenticated, no bearer required)
Test: /federation/peers without PSK auth returns error
Test: /federation/relay delivers message to local broker
Test: /federation/relay with invalid HMAC signature is rejected
Test: /federation/relay without signature is rejected
Test: connection from outside allowed subnet is rejected
Test: peer list sync populates remote peer cache
Test: stale remote peers are cleaned up after 90 seconds
Test: remote peer IDs are prefixed with machine hostname
Test: remote from_ids bypass PID liveness check
Test: local /federation/send-to-remote endpoint requires bearer token (not PSK)
Test: local /federation/connect endpoint requires bearer token (not PSK)
Test: sync error handling — fetch failure does not crash broker
All tests use temporary TLS certs (Ed25519), temp DB, temp token file (same pattern as broker.test.ts)
Tests spin up two federation agents to test bidirectional communication
Minimum 16 tests
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added test for PID liveness bypass on remote from_ids and for PSK-only auth on federation endpoints (verifying bug fixes #1 and #2). Ed25519 certs for test infra.
